summary: Fixed SObjLib data layout and related prototypes by replacing a fake aggregate SObjLibData view with discrete globals/static data that better match the binary: GObjFunc/GObjFuncs tables, filename/string data, padding for the jumptable area, two HSD_Chan initializers, direct OSReport strings, and u16 width/height parameters for HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC. The automated decomp.dev report showed sobjlib .rodata reaching 100%, HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC reaching 100%, and improvements to .data and .sbss.

key_files.role: Main implementation/data fix: removed fake SObjLibData, added concrete data objects, direct strings, HSD_Chan statics, and u16 parameter types.
Header synchronization: included gobj.h and changed HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC width/height parameters from int to u16.
Symbol map correction: split one 0x78 data label into two 0x30 channel objects plus a 0x16 string label.

what_changed: Changed HSD_SObjLib_804D7960 from an extern declaration to a defining u8 object in sobjlib.c.
Removed the local SObjLibData struct that bundled multiple adjacent data objects into one artificial aggregate.
Added HSD_SObjLib_8040C3A0 as a GObjFunc array containing HSD_SObjLib_803A4740 and defined HSD_SObjLib_8040C3A4 as a GObjFuncs table pointing at it.
Added static data for filename = "sobjlib.c" and a zeroed data_pad_jumptable[0x3C].
Replaced accesses through the fake SObjLibData fields with direct objects: OSReport now uses literal messages and HSD_SetupChannel uses two static HSD_Chan initializers.
Added static HSD_Chan lbl_8040C418 for GX_COLOR0 and lbl_8040C448 for GX_ALPHA0 with explicit GX channel fields.
Changed HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC signature from int width/height to u16 width/height in both source and header, allowing simpler bottom/right calculations.
Changed HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC Vec3 setup from extern Vec3 data references to local initializers for eye {0,0,1} and interest {0,0,0}.
Updated GALE01 symbols so lbl_8040C418 is size 0x30, with new lbl_8040C448 size 0x30 and lbl_8040C478 size 0x16 data:string.
smart_moves: Split a contiguous data blob into the actual C objects used by code instead of continuing to model it as one wide synthetic struct.
Kept address-based names for uncertain local data while still giving the compiler typed HSD_Chan and GObjFuncs initializers where the structure was known.
Synchronized source, header, and symbols.txt in one PR, avoiding a partial match where data layout and public prototypes disagree.
Used fixed-width u16 parameters for viewport dimensions, which matched the function's intended conversion behavior better than int placeholders.
Preserved unexplained data space explicitly with data_pad_jumptable[0x3C] rather than deleting or over-typing it.
decomp_lessons: Do not leave independent data objects hidden inside a catch-all struct once object boundaries are known; it can obscure symbol sizes and force unnatural field references.
When a symbol-map label spans multiple real objects, split the label into object-sized entries so later data matching has stable anchors.
Near-match functions can be fixed by correcting parameter widths in both the C definition and header, not just by local expression tweaking.
Direct string literals or static char arrays may be a better match than routing OSReport messages through fields of an invented data struct.
Data-layout cleanup can unlock code matches too: this PR improved sobjlib .data/.sbss and also took HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC to 100%.
naming_conventions: Exported unknown-address globals kept the HSD_SObjLib_8040Cxxx naming style, e.g. HSD_SObjLib_8040C3A0 and HSD_SObjLib_8040C3A4.
Local static data retained lbl_8040Cxxx address-label names when no semantic name was evident, e.g. lbl_8040C418 and lbl_8040C448.
The temporary filler object was named data_pad_jumptable to document its role as data-space padding around the jumptable region.
Function prototypes should use fixed-width Dolphin/project typedefs such as u16 when the original ABI behavior depends on width.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace a fake overlay struct with separate C declarations to match individual data object boundaries and relocations.
Use typed aggregate initializers for HSD_Chan objects so the emitted .data matches channel defaults exactly.
Use u16 width/height parameters to avoid extra casts in HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC and reach the target codegen.
Use local Vec3 initializers for eye and interest instead of extern Vec3 symbols where that matches the function and .rodata layout.
Represent unknown/padding data explicitly with a zeroed byte array when the binary contains a fixed data range that is not yet fully typed.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body were present in the slice.
decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 66.47% (+0.01%, +444 bytes) and matched data 38.80% (+0.00%, +24 bytes).
decomp-dev bot reported 2 new matches: main/sysdolphin/baselib/sobjlib .rodata from 0.00% to 100.00%, and HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC from 99.84% to 100.00%.
decomp-dev bot reported unmatched-item improvements for sobjlib .data (+115 bytes, 0.00% to 48.07%) and .sbss (+4 bytes, 0.00% to 50.00%).
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename lbl_8040C418, lbl_8040C448, lbl_8040C478, or data_pad_jumptable to more semantic sobjlib data names.
Audit data around .data:0x8040C3A0-.data:0x8040C490 to confirm remaining sobjlib/SisLib symbol boundaries after the split.
Search for HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC callers to confirm all width/height arguments are compatible with the u16 prototype.
Compare other baselib modules for fake aggregate data structs that should be split into typed objects and symbol-map labels.
